zh.lifeSummary: 當Millie Anne Griggs於1600年出生在英國素福爾可時，她的父親Thomas Griggs31歲，母親Anne Sterling20歲。她和Richard M. Hackney至少有2個兒子。她去世於1676年1月20日在美國維吉尼亞州米德萊可可利斯丘其享年76歲，埋葬在英國肯特米爾頓 雷吉。

nameMeanings.name: Griggs
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southeastern): from the Middle English personal name Grig, a pet form of Gregory (compare Gregg ), with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Gregory
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from a personal name that was popular throughout Christendom in the Middle Ages. The Greek original, Grēgorios, is a derivative of grēgorein ‘to be awake, to be watchful’. However, the Latin form, Gregorius, came to be associated by folk etymology with grex, gregis ‘flock, herd’, under the influence of the Christian image of the good shepherd. The Greek name was borne in the early Christian centuries by two fathers of the Orthodox Church, Saint Gregory Nazianzene (c. 325–390) and Saint Gregory of Nyssa (c. 331–395), and later by sixteen popes, starting with Gregory the Great (c. 540–604). It was also the name of 3rd- and 4th-century apostles of Armenia. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Italian Gregorio , German, Slovak, and Slovenian Gregor , Polish Grzegorz, Czech Řehoř (see Rehor ), and French Gregoire , and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Polish Grzegorczyk .

nameMeanings.name: Millie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Millicent  , and also, less commonly, of names such as   Mildred  ,   Camilla  , and   Emily .  It is now also a popular given name in its own right.

variants.name: Milly
meanings: From an Old French name, Melisende, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from amal ‘labour’ + swinth ‘strength’. This was the name of a daughter of Charlemagne. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Millie Anne Griggs was born in 1600, in Suffolk, England, her father, Thomas Griggs, was 31 and her mother, Anne Sterling, was 20. She had at least 2 sons with Richard M. Hackney. She died on 20 January 1676, in Christchurch, Middlesex, Virginia, United States, at the age of 76, and was buried in Milton Regis, Kent, England.

Elle est décédée le 20 janvier 1676, à Christchurch, Middlesex, Virginie, États-Unis, à l’âge de 76 ans, et a été enterrée à Milton Regis, Kent, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Millie Anne Griggs(이)가 1600년 영국 수폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Griggs(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Sterling(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 Richard M. Hackney(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1676년 1월 20일 미국 버지니아 주 미들레세스 크리스트c후르치에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 미턴 레지스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Millie Anne Griggs nasceu em 1600, em Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Griggs, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Anne Sterling, tinha 20 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Richard M. Hackney. Ela faleceu em 20 de janeiro de 1676, em Christchurch, Middlesex, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América, com 76 anos, e foi sepultada em Milton Regis, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Millie Anne Griggs родилась в 1600 г. в местности суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Griggs было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Anne Sterling, 20. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына от Richard M. Hackney. Она умерла 20 января 1676 г. в местности крисчурч, миддлесекс, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 76 лет и была похоронена в местности милтон регис, кэнт, Англия.
